message.content:
Update oneshot agent prompt with commit message format requirements

Updated the `kimi-agent-oneshot.json` system prompt to instruct the agent to:
- Start the final message with a one-liner commit message in imperative mode
- Include the fixed issue reference in the one-liner (e.g., "Fix bug (#75)")
- Include a body with a summary of changes
- Reference the fixed issue in the body using "Closes #12345" format

Added an example format showing the proper structure, using issue #75 as requested.

Closes #75
message.reasoning_content:
The JSON has been updated correctly. The new prompt now includes:

1. Clear instructions that the last message serves as a git-commit
2. Instructions to start with a one-liner in imperative mode
3. Instructions to reference the fixed issue in the one-liner
4. Instructions to include a body with a nice summary
5. Instructions to reference the fixed issue with "Closes #12345" in the body
6. An example format showing how to structure the commit message, referencing issue #75 as requested

The JSON is valid and the changes have been made successfully.

contents.userPrompt:
You are an expert Haskell and Bash programmer specialized in writing clean, idiomatic, and efficient Haskell code.
When writing Bash code:
- Avoid bash-specific logic but bash-ism are fine.
- Document required shell tools at the top of the bash script.
When writing Haskell code:
- Use modern Haskell practices (base >= 4.14)
- Prefer pure functions and explicit type signatures
- Use standard libraries when appropriate
- Add clear comments for complex logic
- Follow consistent formatting
Use list-files to explore the project structure, read-file to understand existing code, write-file to create or modify files, and check-code to verify your work.
Always compile code before considering the job done.
Your last message will serve as git-commit, hence you should ensure that you start with a short leading line summarizing the change.
userToolResponses:
----
{
  "tag": "OpenAIAgentDescription",
  "contents": {
  "slug": "kimi-oneshot",
  "flavor": "KimiV1",
  "modelUrl": "https://api.moonshot.ai/v1",
  "apiKeyId": "kimi",
  "modelName": "kimi-k2.5",
  "toolDirectory": "oneshot-tools",
  "announce": "An expert programmer that can write, read, and check Haskell code.",
  "systemPrompt": [
    "You are an expert Haskell and Bash programmer specialized in writing clean, idiomatic, and efficient Haskell code.",
    "When writing Bash code:",
    "- Avoid bash-specific logic but bash-ism are fine.",
    "- Document required shell tools at the top of the bash script.",
    "When writing Haskell code:",
    "- Use modern Haskell practices (base >= 4.14)",
    "- Prefer pure functions and explicit type signatures",
    "- Use standard libraries when appropriate",
    "- Add clear comments for complex logic",
    "- Follow consistent formatting",
    "Use list-files to explore the project structure, read-file to understand existing code, write-file to create or modify files, and check-code to verify your work.",
    "Always compile code before considering the job done.",
    "Your last message will serve as git-commit. Format your final message as follows:",
    "- Start with a one-liner commit message in imperative mode (e.g., 'Fix bug in user authentication')",
    "- The one-liner should reference the fixed issue (e.g., 'Fix bug in user authentication (#12345)')",
    "- Follow with a blank line, then a body with a nice summary of the changes",
    "- In the body, reference the fixed issue using 'Closes #12345' format",
    "",
    "Example format:",
    "Fix critical bug in session handling (#75)",
    "",
    "This commit resolves the memory leak caused by not properly",
    "closing session handles. The fix ensures all resources are",
    "released when a session is terminated.",
    "",
    "Closes #75"
  ]
}
}
